meta.note: Notes for Nicole — Lexi's manager channel (spec §6). Mandatory entry types fire when their triggering condition is met; discretionary entries are observations Lexi thinks Nicole should see. Set status to 'read', 'actioned', or 'dismissed' as appropriate.

entries.subject: Source pattern: domain 'blog.google' appears in 5 recent longlist entries
entries.details: In the past 30 days, 5 longlist entries have at least one source from blog.google. This isn't necessarily wrong — official-source feeds naturally cluster — but it's a signal worth checking, especially if these entries also share authors or were promoted in close temporal proximity.
entries.suggestedAction: Skim the listed entries: are they describing genuinely new vocabulary, or different framings of the same underlying concept from the same source-network? If the latter, consider conflating or holding for additional independent sources before promotion.

entries.subject: Source pattern: domain 'openai.com' appears in 2 recent longlist entries
entries.details: In the past 30 days, 2 longlist entries have at least one source from openai.com. This isn't necessarily wrong — official-source feeds naturally cluster — but it's a signal worth checking, especially if these entries also share authors or were promoted in close temporal proximity.
entries.suggestedAction: Skim the listed entries: are they describing genuinely new vocabulary, or different framings of the same underlying concept from the same source-network? If the latter, consider conflating or holding for additional independent sources before promotion.

entries.subject: Source pattern: domain 'anthropic.com' appears in 8 recent longlist entries
entries.details: In the past 30 days, 8 longlist entries have at least one source from anthropic.com. This isn't necessarily wrong — official-source feeds naturally cluster — but it's a signal worth checking, especially if these entries also share authors or were promoted in close temporal proximity.
entries.suggestedAction: Skim the listed entries: are they describing genuinely new vocabulary, or different framings of the same underlying concept from the same source-network? If the latter, consider conflating or holding for additional independent sources before promotion.

entries.subject: Adoption velocity: 'TPU (Tensor Processing Unit)' hit 2 sources in 1d
entries.details: This entry accumulated 2 sources within 1 day(s) of first sighting. Often legitimate for product launches, but rapid multi-sourcing from a tight source pool can also signal coordinated promotion. Source labels involved: Google AI Blog.
entries.suggestedAction: Cross-check the sources for shared authors or cross-citation. If sources are tightly coupled (same publication network, same author byline), treat as one source for promotion-eligibility purposes.
